children.label: i
children.text: on real property which the veteran owns at the time of application; or

children.label: ii
children.text: as to which the Secretary has incurred actual liability or loss, unless in the event of loss or the incurrence and payment of such liability by the Secretary the resulting indebtedness of the veteran to the United States has been paid in full.

children.label: A
children.text: Each veteran who served on active duty at any time during World War II, the Korean conflict, or the Vietnam era and whose total service was for 90 days or more.

children.label: B
children.text: Each veteran who after , was discharged or released from a period of active duty for a service-connected disability.September 15, 1940

children.label: i
children.text: served after , for a period of more than 180 days and was discharged or released therefrom under conditions other than dishonorable; orJuly 25, 1947

children.label: ii
children.text: has served more than 180 days in active duty status and continues on active duty without a break therein.

children.label: D
children.text: Each veteran who served on active duty for 90 days or more at any time during the Persian Gulf War, other than a veteran ineligible for benefits under this title by reason of .section 5303A(b) of this title

children.label: E
children.text: Each veteran described in .section 3701(b)(5) of this title

children.label: F
children.text: Each veteran who was discharged or released from a period of active duty of 90 days or more by reason of a sole survivorship discharge (as that term is defined in ).section 1174(i) of title 10

children.label: G
children.text: Each individual described in .section 3701(b)(7) of this title
Any unused entitlement of World War II or Korean conflict veterans which expired under provisions of law in effect before , is hereby restored and shall not expire until used.October 23, 1970
A veteran’s entitlement under this chapter shall not be reduced by any entitlement used by the veteran’s spouse which was based upon the provisions of paragraph (3) of .section 3701(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: The property which secured the loan has been disposed of by the veteran or has been destroyed by fire or other natural hazard; and

children.label: B
children.text: the loan has been repaid in full, or the Secretary has been released from liability as to the loan, or if the Secretary has suffered a loss on such loan, the loss has been paid in full.

A veteran-transferee has agreed to assume the outstanding balance on the loan and consented to the use of the veteran-transferee’s entitlement, to the extent that the entitlement of the veteran-transferor had been used originally, in place of the veteran-transferor’s for the guaranteed, insured, or direct loan, and the veteran-transferee otherwise meets the requirements of this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: The loan has been repaid in full; and

children.label: B
children.text: the loan for which the veteran seeks to use entitlement under this chapter is secured by the same property which secured the loan referred to in subparagraph (A) of this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: the loan has been repaid in full and, if the Secretary has suffered a loss on the loan, the loss has been paid in full; or

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary has been released from liability as to the loan and, if the Secretary has suffered a loss on the loan, the loss has been paid in full.

children.label: c
children.text: An honorable discharge shall be deemed to be a certificate of eligibility to apply for a guaranteed loan. Any veteran who does not have a discharge certificate, or who received a discharge other than honorable, may apply to the Secretary for a certificate of eligibility. Upon making a loan guaranteed or insured under this chapter, the lender shall forthwith transmit to the Secretary a report thereon in such detail as the Secretary may, from time to time, prescribe. Where the loan is guaranteed, the Secretary shall provide the lender with a loan guaranty certificate or other evidence of the guaranty. The Secretary shall also endorse on the veteran’s discharge, or eligibility certificate, the amount and type of guaranty used, and the amount, if any, remaining. Nothing in this chapter shall preclude the assignment of any guaranteed loan or the security therefor.

children.label: d
children.text: Housing loans will be automatically guaranteed under this chapter only if made (1) by any Federal land bank, national bank, State bank, private bank, building and loan association, insurance company, credit union, or mortgage and loan company, that is subject to examination and supervision by an agency of the United States or of any State, or (2) by any State, or (3) by any lender approved by the Secretary pursuant to standards established by the Secretary. Any housing loan proposed to be made to a veteran pursuant to this chapter by any lender not of a class specified in the preceding sentence may be guaranteed by the Secretary if the Secretary finds that it is in accord otherwise with the provisions of this chapter.

children.label: e
children.text: The Secretary may at any time upon thirty days’ notice require housing loans to be made by any lender or class of lenders to be submitted to the Secretary for prior approval. No guaranty or insurance liability shall exist with respect to any such loan unless evidence of guaranty or insurance is issued by the Secretary.
ratio of amount of loan to the value of the property;
maturity of loan;
requirement for mortgage or other security;
dignity of lien; or
percentage of assets which may be invested in real estate loans.
